channel.title: SPDK on Xing Lin
channel.description: Recent content in SPDK on Xing Lin
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: SPDK on Intel Optane and Samsung ZSSD
item.description:
Finally got some time to test out Intel Optane SSD and Samsung ZSSD with SPDK. Once again, I compared performance numbers from SPDK with Linux block device. Here are some numbers. The experiments were run in a Ubuntu14.04 server and we tested 4KiB random read performance.

Intel Optane SSD

Metric SPDK /dev/nvme1n1
IOPS 150 K 66.1 K
slat 0.13 usec 2.8 usec
clat 6.2 usec 11.4 usec
lat 6.35 usec 14.32 usec

Samsung ZSSD

Metric SPDK /dev/nvme0n1
IOPS 73.9 K 46.2 K
slat 0.14 usec 3.2 usec
clat 13.0 usec 17.4 usec
lat 13.12 usec 20.73 usec

slat: average submission latency
clat: the time between submission to the kernel and when the IO is complete
lat: total IO latency

item.title: SPDK on Samsung 960 EVO NVMe SSD
item.description:
Today, I started to play with Intel SPDK with a 250GB Samsung 960 EVO NVMe SSD. I was thinking that using SPDK might lead to better performance, than through traditional block device interface. However, using fio with 4KiB random read requests at the queue depth equal to 1, as it turned out, the difference is rather small.

Metric SPDK /dev/nvme0n1
IOPS 12.8 K 11.6 K
slat 0.18 usec –
clat 77.73 usec 84.19 usec
lat 77.91 usec 84.32 usec

slat, clat and lat are average submission latency, submission to IO completion latency and total IO latency. As we can see, this device still have a quite high read latency. The latest Intel Optane DC P4800X SSDs can achieve 10 us read latency, with 550 K IOPS. For this Samsung device, software overhead seems still fine. It would be really interesting to test out for the Intel Optane PC4800X SSDs though.
